title: Discussing the Cartesian Error - Mind Matters
excerpts:
Section Title: Discussing the Cartesian Error
Content:
[Download file](https://op3.dev/e/mindmatters.ai/podcast-download/28873/ep250.mp3?ref=download "Discussing the Cartesian Error ") | [Play in new window](https://op3.dev/e/mindmatters.ai/podcast-download/28873/ep250.mp3?ref=new_window "Discussing the Cartesian Error ") | Duration: 49:40 | Recorded on August 3, 2023
What impact did the ideas of the philosopher René Descartes have on our modern conception of the mind/body problem? In today’s episode, neurosurgeon Michael Egnor digs deeper into his conversation with Dr. Joshua Farris, discussing Thomistic dualism, materialistic explanations for consciousness, and the inevitability of metaphysics.

title: Rene Descartes | Biography, Ideas, Philosophy, 'I Think ...
excerpts:
Section Title: René Descartes > Who was René Descartes? > How did René Descartes die?
Content:
Applying an original system of methodical [doubt](https://www.britannica.com/topic/methodic-doubt) , he dismissed apparent knowledge derived from authority, the senses, and [reason](https://www.britannica.com/topic/reason) and erected new epistemic foundations on the basis of the [intuition](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/intuition) that, when he is [thinking](https://www.britannica.com/topic/thought) , he exists; this he expressed in the dictum “I think, therefore I am” (best known in its Latin formulation, “ [Cogito, ergo sum](https://www.britannica.com/topic/cogito-ergo-sum) ,” though originally written in French, “Je pense, donc je suis”).

title: Mind & Desire Podcast Episode 10 - Rene Descartes On What A Thinking Thing Is
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Mind & Desire Podcast Episode 10 - Rene Descartes On What A Thinking Thing Is
Content:
going beyond just "cogito ergo sum"
[](https://substack.com/@gregorybsadler)
[Gregory B. Sadler](https://substack.com/@gregorybsadler)
Jul 11, 2024
1
Share Transcript
I’m currently teaching on 8-week online course on Rene Descartes’ *Meditations on First Philosophy,* so his philosophy is often on my mind. This goes especially for some of the key passages of the work that created an impression upon me during one reading or another.
Among them is his answer to a question raised in the second Meditation. He has determined that, even if there is an evil demon who turns all of his efforts to deceiving Descartes, he must exist if he is being deceived. And then the question naturally arises: “all right, then what am I?” And Descartes has a short answer to that. “I’m a thinking thing.” But what is that?
